title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
The vicar, Stephen Lane, discusses his views on evil with Hercule Poirot and Colonel Weston, characterizing the deceased, Eleanor Marshall, as a "witch" and an embodiment of evil. Lane provides an alibi, stating he spent the day walking in Saint Pere and visiting its church, where he signed the guest book. When Poirot notes that Marshall was strangled rather than "brought down" by wickedness, Lane denies knowing who committed the act.

The group travels to Elfan Bay, where Chief Inspector Philip has collected various items from a rock on the beach, including scissors, ropes, and a fragment of a high-quality pipe. Poirot observes that Mr. Marshall had previously mentioned a missing pipe. When Poirot asks if he smokes a pipe, Stephen Lennon admits that he does.

At the beach, the Chief Constable explains that the body was found where Lennon had been standing and notes that the tide timing suggests the body arrived at 10:45. Patrick Redfern leads the group to a narrow fissure between boulders at the foot of a cliff, which serves as an entrance to a cave. Weston, Poirot, and Lane enter the cave. While Weston finds nothing suspicious and notes the air is fresh, Poirot detects a subtle fragrance in the air that he recognizes as a perfume used by two specific people. The investigation continues as Poirot spots something on a protruding rock above them, prompting Weston to suggest they check it.
